mechanism_narrative: A sustained three-month decline in nonfarm payrolls signals contracting aggregate wage income and consumption demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of months when PAYEMS declines over a three-month window, or rank information coefficient is positive over any two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held above five percent directly increases debt-service costs for corporations and households, restricting credit creation and compressing equity valuations, which generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds five percent, or rank correlation between FEDFUNDS level and forward returns is positive over a two-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and industrial production confirm a broad demand contraction that is stronger than either signal alone, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of months when both RSAFS and INDPRO decline month-over-month, or the joint interaction term shows no significant negative coefficient (p-value greater than 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices accelerate earnings uncertainty and discount-rate risk when the federal funds rate is above five percent, forcing equity multiple compression and negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with accelerating inflation
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of episodes when FEDFUNDS exceeds five percent and CPIAUCSL is rising, or the interaction term between inflation and rates has a positive sign.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.2 million units signals a deepening residential investment recession that transmits losses to financial intermediaries and construction sectors, dragging the S&P 500 lower over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of months when HOUST is below 1200, or rank information coefficient between HOUST and forward returns is positive during housing-led downturns.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate increase greater than 0.3 percentage points over three months during a period of positive consumer price inflation forces a stagflationary repricing of earnings and multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered episodes when UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points in three months while inflation is positive, or the signal rank information coefficient falls below negative 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and rises in unemployment indicate a manufacturing recession where output cuts and job losses reinforce each other, crushing earnings forecasts and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of quarters when INDPRO declines and UNRATE rises, or the joint signal rank information coefficient is positive over any three-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A negative month-over-month change in retail sales triggers an immediate downgrade of consumer discretionary earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of months when RSAFS declines month-over-month, or rank information coefficient between RSAFS changes and 5-day forward returns is positive.
